blocks.body: Each / Every / All ‚Äî The difference between each, every, and all.

{ "title": "9. Brief synopsis", "items": [ "Each - each separately (individually). Each + singular.", "Every - everyone without exception (as a group). Every + singular.", "All - all together (generality). All + plural.", "Each/Every + singular noun & verb (is, has, does).", "All + plural noun & verb (are, have, do).", "Each of / All of + the/these/my... But NOT 'every of'!", "Each ‚Äî flexible position (before, after, at the end). Every - only before a noun.", "Each - for small groups. Every - for any size." ] }
